transcript.text: Hey, what's up? It's five in the morning. I figure I'll do a video where I talk about college tales. I'll try to do it really quickly, though, because that's like, you know, a whole many bunch of years. Definitely got to boil it down.

Yeah, I went to Fort Lewis College in Durango. It was a really cool college. I probably would have gone to CU if I'd gotten in, but I didn't get in. I think because I dropped out of chemistry class in senior year in high school, because the teacher told me to because I had a bad grade, because it was like a math class, and I didn't want to do any of the tutoring or whatever.

So I didn't, like, officially graduate or something like that. But it didn't really matter, because I was kind of, it was cool to go to Fort Lewis, because it was like 4,000 kids in the whole school, in a town of 13,000 people.

And, like, the college campus is up on a hill, like, so it's like its own town. Like, you look around, and all you see is the college campus. So, and then you go downtown, you ride a hill downtown, you can mountain bike down a trail or ride a hill.
And, uh, the town's really cool. Really, uh, you know, small little town, surrounded by mountains, and, um, oh man, it was an awesome school. It was awesome to be there. It was so much fun.
There was, like, not a whole lot of homework. You know, you didn't have to do a whole lot of homework. So, it was like, you just show up in class, and then you can go mountain biking.
I had, uh, I lived in the dorm room, and my dorm room was, like, 100 feet from where the mountain bike trail started.
And I don't think, it's probably all private property now, but, like, I could ride my mountain bike off in the mountain bike trail, which would be basically single track from 100 feet from my room.
You know, up in some big loop, and do, like, a whole hour worth of riding, you know, on all these different trails, and every day do a different trail.
So, come back to my room, even if I only have, like, an hour and a half between classes, I could get this big-ass mountain bike ride in. It was insane.
And then there's all sorts of other mountain bike trails that I did. Oh, man, now I'm starting to remember.
Reminisce on Durango. I haven't been there since 97. I should go back. Jeez.
Because I did so much mountain biking there.
All these trails I'm thinking about now. Oh, man.
Yeah, because I did a whole bunch of mountain biking in my freshman year.
Um, and, uh, yeah, but, man, I had this tiny little dorm room, man.
It was, like, as big as this room I'm in now.
And I had to share it with this other dude.
